symptoms: Diamond-shaped (spindle-shaped) lesions on leaves with grey/white centers and brown borders.
Lesions on nodes causing the stem to rot and break easily (neck rot).
Infected panicles turn white and remain erect without developing grains.

treatment.organic: Spray Pseudomonas fluorescens liquid formulation @ 10 ml/liter of water or apply Neem seed kernel extract (NSKE 5%). Avoid excessive Nitrogen fertilizers.
treatment.chemical: Spray Tricyclazole 75% WP @ 0.6 g/liter or Isoprothiolane 40% EC @ 1.5 ml/liter of water upon observing early lesions.

prevention: Use certified disease-resistant crop seeds (e.g., Swarna-sub1 or local resistant cultivars).
Ensure recommended spacing to allow airflow and reduce humidity around the crop canopy.
Avoid excess nitrogen fertilizer dosage; split nitrogen application into three distinct stages.
Practice clean cultivation by removing plant debris and burning infected stubble after harvest.

symptoms: Upward or downward curling of leaf margins, making the leaves look cup-shaped.
Thickening of veins on the lower surface of leaves (Enation).
Stunted crop growth and extreme reduction in boll size and boll count.

treatment.organic: Spray Neem Oil @ 5ml/liter with soap solution to manage Whitefly vectors. Install yellow sticky traps (15 traps per acre).
treatment.chemical: Apply systemic insecticides like Acetamiprid 20% SP @ 0.4g/liter or Diafenthiuron 50% WP @ 1.2g/liter to suppress Whitefly populations.

prevention: Eradicate weed hosts (such as Sida acuta and Abutilon) near the farm boundaries.
Grow virus-resistant hybrids developed by state agricultural universities.
Do not cultivate highly susceptible companion crops like bhindi (Okra) near cotton fields.
Enforce seed treatment with Imidacloprid before sowing.

symptoms: Bright yellow, pustule-like stripes running parallel to the leaf veins.
Yellow powder (spores) easily rubs off onto clothing or fingers upon contact.
Leaves dry out rapidly and shrivel under heavy infection, causing severe grain shriveling.

treatment.organic: Aqueous extract of garlic or neem leaf spray can slow down early infection. Ensure immediate potassium fertilization to boost plant immunity.
treatment.chemical: Spray Propiconazole 25% EC (e.g., Tilt) @ 1 ml/liter of water. Under high disease pressure, repeat the spray after 15 days.

prevention: Sow wheat early (before Nov 15) to escape the peak rust spore load coming from hilly tracts.
Cultivate high-yield, rust-resistant varieties such as HD 2967, HD 3086, or DBW 187.
Eradicate alternative grass hosts like Phalaris minor from the field area.
Maintain crop monitoring during cold, foggy, and humid winter mornings.

diseaseName: Chilli Dieback and Fruit Rot (Colletotrichum capsici)
symptoms: Necrosis and progressive drying of branches starting from the branch tips downwards.
Circular, sunken, dark spots on ripe red chillies (Anthracnose).
Infected leaves drop off prematurely and branches turn greyish-white.

treatment.organic: Spray copper oxychloride @ 2.5 g/liter of water or apply Trichoderma viride bio-fungicide to the root zone.
treatment.chemical: Spray Carbendazim 12% + Mancozeb 63% WP (Saaf) @ 2 g/liter or Azoxystrobin 23% SC @ 1 ml/liter when flower buds emerge.

prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds and treat them with Thiram or Captan @ 3g/kg seed before planting.
Promptly prune and burn infected branches to prevent secondary spore propagation.
Maintain excellent drainage to prevent high moisture pockets in the soil.
Avoid sprinkler irrigation; adopt drip irrigation to keep foliage dry.

symptoms: Vibrant green leaf pigment with uniform cellular thickness.
Excellent turgor pressure; no fungal lesions, necrotic tissues, or vein clearing.

treatment.organic: Maintain current organic nutrition. Apply well-composted farmyard manure (FYM) to maintain soil organic carbon.
treatment.chemical: No chemical fungicide or insecticide intervention is required. Save on chemical costs.

prevention: Continue regular crop health scouting once every three days.
Ensure proper balanced N:P:K nutrition according to the Digital Twin soil test values.
Perform scheduled weeding and light hoeing to aerate the root zones.
